description: Fix three test isolation issues: (1) move ensureSdkVersionInManifest() from per-test fixtures to global-setup.ts to eliminate a race condition when parallel workers write to the same source-tree file, (2) deduplicate getConfigDir/getConfigPath in plugin-management.ts by importing from @opentabs-dev/shared, (3) add safety guards preventing tests from accidentally running against the real ~/.opentabs directory, and (4) expand EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN to exclude storybook artifacts that cause flaky ENOENT failures during parallel cpSync.

userStories.title: Move ensureSdkVersionInManifest from fixtures.ts to global-setup.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ensureSdkVersionInManifest to run once before test workers spawn so that parallel workers don't race on writing plugins/e2e-test/dist/tools.json simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: ensureSdkVersionInManifest function is defined in e2e/global-setup.ts and called from the globalSetup() function
ensureSdkVersionInManifest function and all calls to it are removed from e2e/fixtures.ts
The PLUGIN_SDK_PKG_PATH constant in e2e/fixtures.ts is removed (it was only used by ensureSdkVersionInManifest)
global-setup.ts uses Node.js fs APIs (readFileSync, writeFileSync, existsSync) since it runs under Playwright's Node.js runner
readPluginToolNames() in e2e/fixtures.ts no longer calls ensureSdkVersionInManifest
copyE2eTestPlugin() in e2e/fixtures.ts no longer calls ensureSdkVersionInManifest
Comments on readPluginToolNames and copyE2eTestPlugin are updated to explain sdkVersion is patched in global-setup.ts
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
In e2e/fixtures.ts, the function `ensureSdkVersionInManifest` (defined around line 91) patches `plugins/e2e-test/dist/tools.json` in the source tree to add an sdkVersion field. It is called from `readPluginToolNames()` (around line 226) and `copyE2eTestPlugin()` (around line 385). With `fullyParallel: true` and 8 workers in playwright.config.ts, multiple workers can race on this write.

The fix: Move the function into `e2e/global-setup.ts` which runs exactly once in the main process before any test workers are spawned. The function should use Node.js APIs (readFileSync, writeFileSync, existsSync from node:fs) since global-setup runs under Playwright's Node.js runner, not Bun. The SDK version should be read from `platform/plugin-sdk/package.json`. The tools.json path is `plugins/e2e-test/dist/tools.json`.

In global-setup.ts, call ensureSdkVersionInManifest() after the existing REQUIRED_FILES check. Add path constants: `const PLUGIN_SDK_PKG_PATH = resolve('platform/plugin-sdk/package.json');`

In fixtures.ts, remove the `ensureSdkVersionInManifest` function definition, remove the `PLUGIN_SDK_PKG_PATH` constant (line ~74), and remove both call sites. Update the JSDoc comments on `readPluginToolNames` and `copyE2eTestPlugin` to note that sdkVersion is patched once in global-setup.ts.

userStories.title: Deduplicate getConfigDir/getConfigPath in plugin-management.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want plugin-management.ts to import getConfigDir and getConfigPath from @opentabs-dev/shared instead of duplicating them locally, so there is a single source of truth for config path resolution.
acceptanceCriteria: getConfigDir and getConfigPath are imported from @opentabs-dev/shared in plugin-management.ts
The local duplicate definitions of getConfigDir and getConfigPath (with the 'Duplicated from config.ts' comment) are removed from plugin-management.ts
The homedir import from node:os is preserved (it is still used elsewhere in the file for ~/path expansion)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
In platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts around line 352, there are local definitions:
```
const getConfigDir = (): string => Bun.env.OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR || join(homedir(), '.opentabs');
const getConfigPath = (): string => join(getConfigDir(), 'config.json');
```
With a comment saying 'Duplicated from config.ts to avoid circular dependencies'. This is outdated — these functions are in @opentabs-dev/shared, which the file already imports from (line 11: `import { atomicWrite, platformExec } from '@opentabs-dev/shared';`).

The fix: Add `getConfigDir, getConfigPath` to the existing @opentabs-dev/shared import, and delete the local definitions plus the comment block above them. Keep the `homedir` import from node:os — it is still used on line ~398 for `~/` path expansion in removeLocalPlugin.

userStories.title: Add safety guards against accidental host config access in tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests to fail fast if they accidentally target the real ~/.opentabs directory, so that tests never corrupt my host machine's configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/global-setup.ts has an assertNotRealConfigDir() function that throws if OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR points to ~/.opentabs
assertNotRealConfigDir() is called at the start of the globalSetup() function, before other checks
startMcpServer() in e2e/fixtures.ts rejects the promise if configDir resolves to ~/.opentabs
The guard uses path.resolve() for comparison to handle symlinks and trailing slashes
global-setup.ts imports homedir from node:os and join, resolve from node:path
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Two guard locations:

1. In e2e/global-setup.ts: Add a function `assertNotRealConfigDir` that checks `process.env['OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR']`. If set and `resolve(configDir) === resolve(join(homedir(), '.opentabs'))`, throw an Error explaining the issue. Call this at the start of `globalSetup()`. Import `homedir` from `node:os` and `join, resolve` from `node:path` (global-setup.ts already imports `resolve` from `node:path` — add `join` to that import and add the `homedir` import).

2. In e2e/fixtures.ts, in the `startMcpServer` function (around line 510): At the top of the `new Promise` callback, add a guard that checks `path.resolve(configDir) === path.resolve(path.join(os.homedir(), '.opentabs'))`. If true, call `reject(new Error(...))` and `return`. The function already has `os` and `path` imported.

userStories.title: Expand EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN to exclude storybook and non-runtime artifacts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the extension copy filter to exclude .storybook, storybook-static, build scripts, package.json, and CLAUDE.md so that cpSync does not fail with ENOENT when storybook-static is being modified by another process during parallel test runs.
acceptanceCriteria: EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN in platform/shared/src/index.ts matches .storybook and storybook-static directories
EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN matches build-*.ts files (e.g., build-extension.ts, build-side-panel.ts)
EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN matches package.json at root level
EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN matches CLAUDE.md at root level
EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN still excludes node_modules, src, .git, and tsconfig* (existing behavior preserved)
EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN does NOT match dist, manifest.json, icons, side-panel, or offscreen (runtime files preserved)
The comment above the pattern is updated to list all exclusion categories

Validation tests in platform/shared/src/validation.test.ts are updated with test cases for .storybook, storybook-static, build-extension.ts, build-side-panel.ts, package.json, CLAUDE.md, offscreen, and offscreen/offscreen.html

createExtensionCopy in e2e/fixtures.ts uses the same regex pattern (inline, with a comment referencing EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN as the canonical source) and uses path.relative for comparison like extension-install.ts does
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
